steps.title: Use the one-solution condition
steps.content:
After clearing fractions, the equation is quadratic in $x$. The question says it has exactly one solution, so the quadratic must touch the $x$-axis once instead of crossing twice. That happens when the discriminant is $0$. For $cx^2+96x-96=0$:
$$96^2-4(c)(-96)=0$$
